Primary Text: The system shall implement a modular microservices architecture where each service is independently deployable.
Name: The system shall implement a modular microservices architecture where each service is independently deployable.

Primary Text: Each microservice shall expose its API exclusively via the API Gateway; direct service-to-service calls shall not be permitted in production.
Name: Each microservice shall expose its API exclusively via the API Gateway; direct service-to-service calls shall not be permitted in production.

Primary Text: The API Gateway shall enforce rate limiting per authenticated client at a default of 1000 requests per minute, configurable per client.
Name: The API Gateway shall enforce rate limiting per authenticated client at a default of 1000 requests per minute, configurable per client.

Primary Text: Rate limit counters shall be maintained in a distributed cache shared across all API Gateway instances to ensure consistent enforcement.
Name: Rate limit counters shall be maintained in a distributed cache shared across all API Gateway instances to ensure consistent enforcement.

Primary Text: The distributed cache shall use a Redis Cluster with a minimum of 3 primary nodes and 1 replica per primary for high availability.
Name: The distributed cache shall use a Redis Cluster with a minimum of 3 primary nodes and 1 replica per primary for high availability.

Primary Text: Redis Cluster configuration parameters including node count, replication factor, and timeout values shall be defined in the infrastructure-as-code repository.
Name: Redis Cluster configuration parameters including node count, replication factor, and timeout values shall be defined in the infrastructure-as-code repository.

Primary Text: Each microservice shall publish structured logs in JSON format to the centralised logging platform.
Name: Each microservice shall publish structured logs in JSON format to the centralised logging platform.

Primary Text: Log entries shall include: service name, instance ID, timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC), log level, trace ID, span ID, and message.
Name: Log entries shall include: service name, instance ID, timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC), log level, trace ID, span ID, and message.

Primary Text: The trace ID and span ID fields shall conform to the W3C Trace Context specification.
Name: The trace ID and span ID fields shall conform to the W3C Trace Context specification.

Primary Text: All services shall propagate the W3C traceparent and tracestate HTTP headers to downstream service calls.
Name: All services shall propagate the W3C traceparent and tracestate HTTP headers to downstream service calls.

Primary Text: Header propagation shall be implemented using the OpenTelemetry SDK and shall not require manual implementation in individual services.
Name: Header propagation shall be implemented using the OpenTelemetry SDK and shall not require manual implementation in individual services.

Primary Text: Microservices shall be stateless; session state shall be stored exclusively in the shared session store.
Name: Microservices shall be stateless; session state shall be stored exclusively in the shared session store.

Primary Text: The shared session store shall be backed by Redis with a session TTL of 1800 seconds (30 minutes).
Name: The shared session store shall be backed by Redis with a session TTL of 1800 seconds (30 minutes).

Primary Text: Session data shall be encrypted at rest within Redis using the AES-256-GCM algorithm.
Name: Session data shall be encrypted at rest within Redis using the AES-256-GCM algorithm.

Primary Text: The system shall implement a data model supporting the full lifecycle of engineering requirements from initial capture to archival.
Name: The system shall implement a data model supporting the full lifecycle of engineering requirements from initial capture to archival.

Primary Text: The data model shall represent requirements as versioned entities where each state change produces an immutable version record.
Name: The data model shall represent requirements as versioned entities where each state change produces an immutable version record.

Primary Text: Version records shall be append-only and shall not be modifiable or deletable by any user role including Administrator.
Name: Version records shall be append-only and shall not be modifiable or deletable by any user role including Administrator.

Primary Text: The append-only constraint shall be enforced at the database level using row-level security policies.
Name: The append-only constraint shall be enforced at the database level using row-level security policies.

Primary Text: Row-level security policies shall be tested as part of the database migration test suite run before each deployment.
Name: Row-level security policies shall be tested as part of the database migration test suite run before each deployment.

Primary Text: Migration test failures related to row-level security shall block the deployment pipeline and trigger a P1 alert.
Name: Migration test failures related to row-level security shall block the deployment pipeline and trigger a P1 alert.

Primary Text: The system shall store traceability links as first-class entities with their own audit trail independent of the linked artefacts.
Name: The system shall store traceability links as first-class entities with their own audit trail independent of the linked artefacts.

Primary Text: Each traceability link entity shall record: source artefact ID and version, target artefact ID and version, link type, creating user, and creation timestamp.
Name: Each traceability link entity shall record: source artefact ID and version, target artefact ID and version, link type, creating user, and creation timestamp.

Primary Text: When a linked artefact is modified, the system shall create a new link entity referencing the new artefact version and mark the previous link entity as superseded.
Name: When a linked artefact is modified, the system shall create a new link entity referencing the new artefact version and mark the previous link entity as superseded.

Primary Text: Superseded link entities shall be retained in the database and shall be accessible via the link history view.
Name: Superseded link entities shall be retained in the database and shall be accessible via the link history view.

Primary Text: The link history view shall display superseded links with a visual indicator distinguishing them from current active links.
Name: The link history view shall display superseded links with a visual indicator distinguishing them from current active links.

Primary Text: The system shall support soft deletion of requirements artefacts; deleted artefacts shall be marked as deleted and excluded from standard views but retained in the database.
Name: The system shall support soft deletion of requirements artefacts; deleted artefacts shall be marked as deleted and excluded from standard views but retained in the database.

Primary Text: Soft-deleted artefacts shall be restorable by users with the Approver role or higher within 90 days of deletion.
Name: Soft-deleted artefacts shall be restorable by users with the Approver role or higher within 90 days of deletion.

Primary Text: After 90 days, soft-deleted artefacts shall be automatically archived to cold storage and shall no longer be restorable through the application interface.
Name: After 90 days, soft-deleted artefacts shall be automatically archived to cold storage and shall no longer be restorable through the application interface.

Primary Text: The system shall provide machine-readable interfaces supporting automated test toolchain integration.
Name: The system shall provide machine-readable interfaces supporting automated test toolchain integration.

Primary Text: The system shall expose a REST API conforming to OpenAPI Specification 3.1 with a machine-readable schema available at /api/v1/openapi.json.
Name: The system shall expose a REST API conforming to OpenAPI Specification 3.1 with a machine-readable schema available at /api/v1/openapi.json.

Primary Text: All API endpoints shall implement HTTP standard methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) with semantics conforming to RFC 7231.
Name: All API endpoints shall implement HTTP standard methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) with semantics conforming to RFC 7231.

Primary Text: Mutating operations (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) shall require a CSRF token passed in the X-CSRF-Token request header for browser-based clients.
Name: Mutating operations (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) shall require a CSRF token passed in the X-CSRF-Token request header for browser-based clients.

Primary Text: The CSRF token shall be a cryptographically random 128-bit value generated per user session.
Name: The CSRF token shall be a cryptographically random 128-bit value generated per user session.

Primary Text: CSRF token generation shall use the system's cryptographically secure random number generator (CSRNG) and shall not use Math.random() or equivalent.
Name: CSRF token generation shall use the system's cryptographically secure random number generator (CSRNG) and shall not use Math.random() or equivalent.

Primary Text: The system shall support OAuth 2.0 authorisation code flow with PKCE for API client authentication.
Name: The system shall support OAuth 2.0 authorisation code flow with PKCE for API client authentication.

Primary Text: OAuth 2.0 tokens shall have a maximum lifetime of 3600 seconds (1 hour) for access tokens and 86400 seconds (24 hours) for refresh tokens.
Name: OAuth 2.0 tokens shall have a maximum lifetime of 3600 seconds (1 hour) for access tokens and 86400 seconds (24 hours) for refresh tokens.

Primary Text: Refresh token rotation shall be enforced — each use of a refresh token shall invalidate it and issue a new refresh token.
Name: Refresh token rotation shall be enforced — each use of a refresh token shall invalidate it and issue a new refresh token.

Primary Text: Refresh token reuse detection shall trigger an immediate invalidation of all tokens in the affected session and an alert to the security operations team.
Name: Refresh token reuse detection shall trigger an immediate invalidation of all tokens in the affected session and an alert to the security operations team.

Primary Text: The reuse detection alert shall include: user ID, token ID, timestamp of original issuance, and timestamp of the reuse attempt.
Name: The reuse detection alert shall include: user ID, token ID, timestamp of original issuance, and timestamp of the reuse attempt.

Primary Text: The system shall implement pagination for all list endpoints using cursor-based pagination with a default page size of 50 and a maximum of 500.
Name: The system shall implement pagination for all list endpoints using cursor-based pagination with a default page size of 50 and a maximum of 500.

Primary Text: Cursor tokens shall be opaque to the client and shall encode the sort key and direction used by the query.
Name: Cursor tokens shall be opaque to the client and shall encode the sort key and direction used by the query.

Primary Text: Cursor tokens shall have a validity period of 10 minutes to prevent use of stale pagination state.
Name: Cursor tokens shall have a validity period of 10 minutes to prevent use of stale pagination state.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall meet quality and compliance requirements defined by the organisation's quality management system.
Name: The system shall meet quality and compliance requirements defined by the organisation's quality management system.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall maintain an uptime of 99.9% measured over any rolling 30-day calendar period.
Name: The system shall maintain an uptime of 99.9% measured over any rolling 30-day calendar period.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Uptime shall be measured from the perspective of the external health check endpoint using a third-party monitoring service.
Name: Uptime shall be measured from the perspective of the external health check endpoint using a third-party monitoring service.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Planned maintenance windows of up to 4 hours per month shall be excluded from uptime calculations if communicated 72 hours in advance.
Name: Planned maintenance windows of up to 4 hours per month shall be excluded from uptime calculations if communicated 72 hours in advance.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Maintenance window notices shall be published on the organisation's service status page and sent to all registered users via email.
Name: Maintenance window notices shall be published on the organisation's service status page and sent to all registered users via email.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The service status page shall be hosted independently of the main system so that it remains accessible during system maintenance.
Name: The service status page shall be hosted independently of the main system so that it remains accessible during system maintenance.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall retain complete audit logs for a minimum of 7 years in compliance with organisational record retention policy RET-POL-003.
Name: The system shall retain complete audit logs for a minimum of 7 years in compliance with organisational record retention policy RET-POL-003.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Audit logs shall record all create, read, update, and delete operations on requirement artefacts performed by any user or automated process.
Name: Audit logs shall record all create, read, update, and delete operations on requirement artefacts performed by any user or automated process.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Read operations on requirement artefacts with Confidentiality classification of 'Restricted' or above shall be included in the audit log.
Name: Read operations on requirement artefacts with Confidentiality classification of 'Restricted' or above shall be included in the audit log.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The audit log record for a read operation shall include: user ID, artefact ID, artefact classification, and timestamp.
Name: The audit log record for a read operation shall include: user ID, artefact ID, artefact classification, and timestamp.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Audit log records shall not include the full content of the accessed artefact to avoid duplication of controlled content in the audit system.
Name: Audit log records shall not include the full content of the accessed artefact to avoid duplication of controlled content in the audit system.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall pass an independent security penetration test with no Critical or High findings before each major release.
Name: The system shall pass an independent security penetration test with no Critical or High findings before each major release.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The penetration test scope shall cover the API, the web application, and the infrastructure layer.
Name: The penetration test scope shall cover the API, the web application, and the infrastructure layer.

section: PART IV — QUALITY AND COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Penetration test remediation shall be completed and re-tested before the release is approved for production.
Name: Penetration test remediation shall be completed and re-tested before the release is approved for production.

Primary Text: The system shall support operational procedures including deployment, monitoring, incident response, and disaster recovery.
Name: The system shall support operational procedures including deployment, monitoring, incident response, and disaster recovery.

Primary Text: The system shall support zero-downtime deployments using a rolling update strategy.
Name: The system shall support zero-downtime deployments using a rolling update strategy.

Primary Text: The rolling update shall replace no more than 25% of running instances simultaneously.
Name: The rolling update shall replace no more than 25% of running instances simultaneously.

Primary Text: Each replacement instance shall pass the readiness probe before the next batch of instances is updated.
Name: Each replacement instance shall pass the readiness probe before the next batch of instances is updated.

Primary Text: The readiness probe shall verify database connectivity, cache connectivity, and identity provider connectivity before returning a ready state.
Name: The readiness probe shall verify database connectivity, cache connectivity, and identity provider connectivity before returning a ready state.

Primary Text: The maximum time allowed for the readiness probe to complete is 10 seconds; probe timeout shall be a deployment configuration parameter.
Name: The maximum time allowed for the readiness probe to complete is 10 seconds; probe timeout shall be a deployment configuration parameter.

Primary Text: The system shall have a documented disaster recovery plan with a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour.
Name: The system shall have a documented disaster recovery plan with a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour.

Primary Text: The disaster recovery plan shall be tested with a full failover exercise at least once every 12 months.
Name: The disaster recovery plan shall be tested with a full failover exercise at least once every 12 months.

Primary Text: The results of each DR exercise shall be documented and reviewed by the operations team and the system owner.
Name: The results of each DR exercise shall be documented and reviewed by the operations team and the system owner.

Primary Text: Any failures identified during the DR exercise shall be tracked as defects in the project issue tracker and resolved before the next exercise.
Name: Any failures identified during the DR exercise shall be tracked as defects in the project issue tracker and resolved before the next exercise.

Primary Text: DR exercise defects shall be treated with the same priority as production incidents of equivalent severity.
Name: DR exercise defects shall be treated with the same priority as production incidents of equivalent severity.

Primary Text: The system shall expose metrics in Prometheus exposition format at the /metrics endpoint, accessible only from within the internal monitoring network.
Name: The system shall expose metrics in Prometheus exposition format at the /metrics endpoint, accessible only from within the internal monitoring network.

Primary Text: Exposed metrics shall include: request rate, error rate, latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99), and active connection count per service.
Name: Exposed metrics shall include: request rate, error rate, latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99), and active connection count per service.

Primary Text: Custom business metrics shall also be exposed including: active user sessions, requirements created per hour, and baseline creation count.
Name: Custom business metrics shall also be exposed including: active user sessions, requirements created per hour, and baseline creation count.

Primary Text: Metric names shall follow Prometheus naming conventions with the prefix 'reqmgmt_' for all application-specific metrics.
Name: Metric names shall follow Prometheus naming conventions with the prefix 'reqmgmt_' for all application-specific metrics.

Primary Text: A complete list of exposed metrics and their descriptions shall be maintained in the operations runbook and updated with each release.
Name: A complete list of exposed metrics and their descriptions shall be maintained in the operations runbook and updated with each release.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall provide a configurable notification and alerting subsystem supporting email, webhook, and in-application channels.
Name: The system shall provide a configurable notification and alerting subsystem supporting email, webhook, and in-application channels.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall allow project administrators to configure notification rules based on requirement state changes.
Name: The system shall allow project administrators to configure notification rules based on requirement state changes.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Notification rules shall support conditions based on attribute values, link events, and baseline creation events.
Name: Notification rules shall support conditions based on attribute values, link events, and baseline creation events.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Multiple conditions within a single notification rule shall be evaluated using configurable AND or OR logic.
Name: Multiple conditions within a single notification rule shall be evaluated using configurable AND or OR logic.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Notification rule evaluation shall occur within 30 seconds of the triggering event under normal system load.
Name: Notification rule evaluation shall occur within 30 seconds of the triggering event under normal system load.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: If notification delivery fails, the system shall retry delivery using exponential backoff up to a maximum of 3 attempts before marking the notification as failed.
Name: If notification delivery fails, the system shall retry delivery using exponential backoff up to a maximum of 3 attempts before marking the notification as failed.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall provide an in-application notification centre displaying unread notifications to the authenticated user.
Name: The system shall provide an in-application notification centre displaying unread notifications to the authenticated user.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Unread notification count shall be displayed as a badge on the notification icon in the application header.
Name: Unread notification count shall be displayed as a badge on the notification icon in the application header.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The badge count shall update in real time without requiring a page reload, using a WebSocket or server-sent events connection.
Name: The badge count shall update in real time without requiring a page reload, using a WebSocket or server-sent events connection.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall allow users to configure their individual notification preferences, overriding project-level defaults.
Name: The system shall allow users to configure their individual notification preferences, overriding project-level defaults.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: User notification preferences shall be stored per project and shall be migrated when a user is transferred between projects.
Name: User notification preferences shall be stored per project and shall be migrated when a user is transferred between projects.

section: PART VI — NOTIFICATION AND ALERTING REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Notification preference migration shall preserve all user-defined settings and shall not revert to project defaults.
Name: Notification preference migration shall preserve all user-defined settings and shall not revert to project defaults.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall provide full-text search across all requirement artefacts within a project accessible to the authenticated user.
Name: The system shall provide full-text search across all requirement artefacts within a project accessible to the authenticated user.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Full-text search shall index the Title, Description, and all string-type custom attribute values of each requirement artefact.
Name: Full-text search shall index the Title, Description, and all string-type custom attribute values of each requirement artefact.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The search index shall be updated within 5 seconds of a requirement artefact being created or modified.
Name: The search index shall be updated within 5 seconds of a requirement artefact being created or modified.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Search index updates shall be processed asynchronously and shall not block the API response for the triggering write operation.
Name: Search index updates shall be processed asynchronously and shall not block the API response for the triggering write operation.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: If the search index update fails, the failure shall be logged and retried up to 5 times before the artefact is flagged for manual re-indexing.
Name: If the search index update fails, the failure shall be logged and retried up to 5 times before the artefact is flagged for manual re-indexing.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The manual re-indexing flag shall be visible in the system administration interface alongside the artefact identifier and the failure timestamp.
Name: The manual re-indexing flag shall be visible in the system administration interface alongside the artefact identifier and the failure timestamp.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall support saved searches that can be named, stored per user, and re-executed on demand.
Name: The system shall support saved searches that can be named, stored per user, and re-executed on demand.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Saved searches shall support parametric substitution allowing date-range parameters to be updated at execution time without editing the saved query.
Name: Saved searches shall support parametric substitution allowing date-range parameters to be updated at execution time without editing the saved query.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Parametric substitution shall support relative date expressions including 'today', 'last 7 days', and 'last 30 days'.
Name: Parametric substitution shall support relative date expressions including 'today', 'last 7 days', and 'last 30 days'.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall provide a bulk export of all search results in CSV format containing a configurable subset of requirement attributes.
Name: The system shall provide a bulk export of all search results in CSV format containing a configurable subset of requirement attributes.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The bulk export shall be processed asynchronously for result sets exceeding 500 artefacts and delivered to the user via a download link.
Name: The bulk export shall be processed asynchronously for result sets exceeding 500 artefacts and delivered to the user via a download link.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: Download links for async bulk exports shall be valid for 24 hours after generation and shall require authentication to access.
Name: Download links for async bulk exports shall be valid for 24 hours after generation and shall require authentication to access.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall notify the requesting user by email when an asynchronous bulk export is ready for download.
Name: The system shall notify the requesting user by email when an asynchronous bulk export is ready for download.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The download notification email shall include the export parameters, the record count, and the expiry time of the download link.
Name: The download notification email shall include the export parameters, the record count, and the expiry time of the download link.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The system shall provide an advanced filter panel supporting multi-attribute filtering on the requirements list view.
Name: The system shall provide an advanced filter panel supporting multi-attribute filtering on the requirements list view.

section: PART VII — SEARCH AND DISCOVERY REQUIREMENTS
Primary Text: The advanced filter panel shall retain the last applied filter set per user per project across browser sessions using persistent local storage.
Name: The advanced filter panel shall retain the last applied filter set per user per project across browser sessions using persistent local storage.
